profile.name: Sabir Jan
profile.title: AI & Machine Learning Researcher
profile.bio: Computer Engineering graduate specializing in Agentic AI, Deep Learning, and Computer Vision. Architect of EmotiFi (contactless Wi-Fi emotion recognition) and stateful multi-agent educational systems.

projects.title: EmotiFi
projects.subtitle: Final Year Research Project
projects.description: A privacy-preserving emotion recognition system using Wi-Fi Channel State Information and deep learning. The project transforms CSI data into spectrograms and classifies emotional states using neural networks.
projects.longDescription: EmotiFi is a cutting-edge research project that leverages Wi-Fi Channel State Information (CSI) to detect human emotions without wearable sensors. By analyzing the distortions in Wi-Fi signals caused by human presence and movement, the system extracts unique biometric signatures. The project transforms CSI data into spectrograms and classifies emotional states using neural networks.

presentationSlides.title: Full Presentation
presentationSlides.description: Comprehensive 16-slide presentation with detailed research findings

visualEvidence.title: Viewing spectrogram and detecting Emotion real time

projects.title: DanReality: Heritage Dance AI
projects.subtitle: Creative Tech // Cultural Arts
projects.description: Holistic fusion of Hunza heritage dance and AI designed to promote mental wellness through real-time gesture feedback and affective synchronization.
projects.longDescription: DanReality is an experimental research project developed by JanBroz, our creative laboratory where we explore the intersection of Hunza heritage and modern performance. While JanBroz serves as our dedicated studio for cultural storytelling and artistic experimentation, DanReality is the scalable research venture born from these efforts, aimed at transforming dance into a scientific discipline for mental wellness. Our project utilizes computer vision (MediaPipe) to track traditional dance movements in real-time, providing immediate feedback on posture, rhythm, and emotional expression. By leveraging the tech stack including Unity 3D, MediaPipe, React, Node.js, Socket.io, Three.js, and Blender we are building a foundation to engineer 'Affective Kinematic Synchronization'—allowing AI to not just mimic movement, but to anticipate and 'feel' the emotional intent of human partners to improve global cognitive and emotional well-being.
projects.researchDescription: Holistic fusion of Hunza heritage dance and AI designed to promote mental wellness through real-time gesture feedback and affective synchronization. Moving beyond camera-based mimicry, DanReality is engineering empathy into motion, developing systems that learn to anticipate human rhythm and intent to serve as a companion for mental health support and physical therapy.
projects.abstractSpecification: DanReality is an experimental research project developed by JanBroz, our creative laboratory where we explore the intersection of Hunza heritage and modern performance. While JanBroz serves as our dedicated studio for cultural storytelling and artistic experimentation, DanReality is the scalable research venture born from these efforts, aimed at transforming dance into a scientific discipline for mental wellness. Our project utilizes computer vision (MediaPipe) to track traditional dance movements in real-time, providing immediate feedback on posture, rhythm, and emotional expression. By leveraging a tech stack including Unity, React, Three.js, and Node.js, we are building a foundation to engineer 'Affective Kinematic Synchronization'—allowing AI to not just mimic movement, but to anticipate and 'feel' the emotional intent of human partners to improve global cognitive and emotional well-being.

projects.title: AI Study Assistant
projects.subtitle: AI EDUCATION SYSTEM // RESEARCH
projects.description: An adaptive learning platform built with LangGraph, FastAPI, Gemini, SQLite, and Streamlit. Features include topic explanation, quiz generation, automated evaluation, recall learning, progress analytics, and adaptive recommendations.
projects.longDescription: The AI Study Assistant is an adaptive learning platform built with LangGraph, FastAPI, Gemini, SQLite, and Streamlit. Features include topic explanation, quiz generation, automated evaluation, recall learning, progress analytics, and adaptive recommendations. Multiple AI agents collaborate through LangGraph workflows to provide personalized learning experiences. Student performance is stored in SQLite and analyzed to recommend future quiz difficulty levels.

gallery.caption: Student Dashboard and Topic Selection Interface
Adaptive Quiz Generation and Assessment Mode
Quiz History and Spaced Repetition Recall Panel
System Architecture and Interactive Tutoring Design

features.name: Topic Explanation
features.description: Generate concise educational explanations for any study topic using Gemini.

features.name: Quiz Generation
features.description: Create dynamic quizzes with adjustable difficulty levels and configurable question counts.

features.name: Quiz Evaluation
features.description: Automatically evaluate student answers and provide detailed feedback.

features.name: Quiz Recall
features.description: Retrieve and review previously generated quizzes from persistent storage.

features.name: Progress Dashboard
features.description: Visualize learning progress using performance metrics and score history.

features.name: Adaptive Learning
features.description: Recommend future quiz difficulty levels based on historical performance.

systemArchitecture: LangGraph Orchestration: Coordinates 4+ specialized AI agents for structured tutoring, quiz creation, grading, and adaptive recommendations.
FastAPI Service Layer: High-performance backend API layer serving lightweight frontends with low-latency learning delivery.
Gemini API Reasoning: Advanced natural language synthesis powering rich concept explanation and deep contextual evaluation.
SQLite Storage: Securely persists active learning status, quiz history logs, student metrics, and evaluation history.
Streamlit & Pandas Interface: Desktop application featuring interactive study workspaces, past record retrieval, and streak performance tracking.
educationalImpact: Multi-Agent Advantage: Collaborating agents provide highly structured, specific feedback loops compared to simple conversational chatbots.
Adaptive Tutoring: Continuous performance assessments adjust feedback and suggestion paths to support students of all skill levels.

projects.title: Line Following Robot
projects.subtitle: Embedded Systems // PID Control
projects.description: Autonomous robot utilizing infra-red sensors and PID control for high-speed line tracking and obstacle avoidance.
projects.longDescription: A robust embedded systems project featuring a line-following robot capable of navigating complex tracks with variable speeds. Implemented using Arduino/C++, featuring real-time sensor calibration and optimized control loops (PID).

details: Completed a rigorous 3-month advanced AI & Deep Learning specialization, clearing official SLAC engineering evaluations.
Engineered KhudiChat, a virtual avatar driven by Generative AI utilizing MediaPipe, OpenCV, Stable Diffusion, and GAN-based expression synthesis.
Developed Agentic AI applications using transformer networks and autonomous multi-agent workflows integrated into production sprints.
Built production-grade, scalable RAG architectures and conversational backends utilizing Python, LangChain, and LangGraph.
Designed state-driven crisis tracking flows with integrated safety fallbacks and interactive Streamlit interfaces.
Collaborated on multi-agent workflow optimizations, semantic search pipelines, and vector databases using ChromaDB.

education.details: Focus Areas: Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, and Edge Embedded Systems. Core Achievements: Lead Developer for EmotiFi (Contactless Emotion Recognition via Wi-Fi CSI).
Graduated with a focus on Advanced Mathematics and Physics.
